scenario: Aurora Logistics ships 4000 orders per day in Camunda 8.8 on the order-process model. After repeated XOR gateway incidents during a 2 hour window at the logistics provider, the operations team must recall the exact data type their order-process model requires for orderValue so the gateway condition stops failing on new instances.
question: Which data type must orderValue carry so the XOR gateway condition orderValue>=100 evaluates without raising an incident?

options.text: The orderValue variable must be a JSON object containing a nested amount field so the XOR gateway condition orderValue>=100 can read the numeric amount of the order from the payload on the broker
The orderValue variable must be a string formatted as an ISO 4217 currency literal so the XOR gateway condition orderValue>=100 can compare it against the 100 threshold defined on the gateway model
The orderValue variable must be a boolean flag so the XOR gateway condition orderValue>=100 can route the instance along the higher-value sequence flow for every order processed by the provider
The orderValue variable must be an integer so the XOR gateway condition orderValue>=100 can compare numeric values and route the order instance along the correct outgoing sequence flow on the model

a.text: Incorrect. The documented model requires orderValue to be an integer; the example incident message Cannot compare values of different types: STRING and INTEGER confirms the gateway compares orderValue itself as an integer, not a nested object field.

b.text: Incorrect. A string value is exactly what causes the documented incident; the engine cannot compare a STRING against an INTEGER, so orderValue must be an integer for the gateway condition orderValue>=100 to evaluate.

c.text: Incorrect. A boolean cannot satisfy a numeric comparison like orderValue>=100; the documented model requires an integer orderValue so the XOR gateway can evaluate the threshold and route the instance.

d.text: Correct. The Operate guide shows that storing orderValue as a string raises an incident because the engine cannot compare STRING and INTEGER, so the operator removes the quotation marks to make orderValue an integer for the gateway.

explanation: The order-process XOR gateway condition orderValue>=100 requires orderValue to be an integer so the engine can compare numeric values; a string value raises an incident with the message Cannot compare values of different types: STRING and INTEGER.
